name: Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55)
name_original: Embalse de Alba — circular por la Ermita de la Virgen de Oca
description: Hike the moderate Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55) through the Oca gorge and Montes de Oca forests, covering 6.5 miles (10.5 km).
region_intro: Hiking around Villafranca Montes De Oca offers diverse landscapes within the Montes de Oca mountain range. The region features the Oca River gorge, extensive pine and oak forests, and significant elevation changes, including the Alto de la Pedraja. These varied terrains provide a range of hiking experiences, from river valleys to mountain slopes. The area is also a key section of the historic Camino de Santiago. Best hiking trails around Villafranca Montes De Oca The most popular hiking route is Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55), a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows parts of the French Way and skirts the Oca River gorge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino de Santiago — circular by San Juan de Ortega, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. This trail offers a circular experience of the historic pilgrimage route through forests. Local hikers also love the Monte de la Pedraja Memorial – San Juan de Ortega loop from Valdefuentes (Fuente del Carnero), a 15.5 miles (24.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 6 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Villafranca Montes De Oca is defined by mountainous backdrops, river gorges, and dense pine and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Villafranca Montes De Oca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Villafranca Montes De Oca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villafranca Montes De Oca?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails in the Villafranca Montes De Oca region, offering a wide variety of experiences. These include routes through the Montes de Oca mountain range, along the Oca River gorge, and through dense pine and oak forests.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Villafranca Montes De Oca?
A: Hikers in Villafranca Montes De Oca will encounter diverse landscapes, from the mountainous terrain of the Montes de Oca to the scenic gorge of the Oca River. The area is rich in colorful forests, including old oak and pine woodlands, and features significant elevation changes, such as the climb to the Alto de la Pedraja, which offers panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Villafranca Montes De Oca?
A: Yes, the region offers several circular routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041671835/alto-oca-trail-pr-bu-55" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55)&lt;/a&gt;, which follows parts of the French Way and skirts the Oca River gorge. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042287038/camino-de-santiago-circular-by-san-juan-de-ortega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago — circular by San Juan de Ortega&lt;/a&gt;, providing a circular experience through historic pilgrimage paths and forests.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villafranca Montes De Oca?
A: The best months for hiking in Villafranca Montes De Oca are generally spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild, and the natural landscapes, especially the forests, are particularly vibrant. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring colder temperatures and snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in the area?
A: Yes, among the 52 available routes, there are 9 easy trails that are suitable for families. These routes often feature gentler terrain and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable experience for all ages. Look for paths that explore the less challenging sections of the forests or riverbanks.

Q: Can I find hikes with waterfalls in Villafranca Montes De Oca?
A: Yes, the region offers natural attractions including waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6315804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altuzarra Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the natural monuments around Villafranca Montes De Oca, providing a refreshing highlight during your hike.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: Many trails in Villafranca Montes De Oca pass by significant historical and cultural sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3852877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery and Church of Saint John of Ortega&lt;/a&gt;, an authentic landmark on the Camino de Santiago. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5717943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de la Pedraja Memorial&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4465826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artistic Oasis of the Camino de Santiago&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Villafranca Montes De Oca?
A: The hiking routes in Villafranca Montes De Oca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical significance of the Camino de Santiago sections, and the peaceful atmosphere of the extensive forests and river gorges.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance hikes in the area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are several difficult long-distance routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesón Alba – Climb to Monte de la Pedraja loop from Villafranca Montes de Oca&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 30 km with significant elevation gain, offering a strenuous but rewarding experience through varied terrain.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Villafranca Montes De Oca?
A: Yes, Villafranca Montes De Oca is accustomed to hikers, particularly due to its location on the Camino de Santiago. You can typically find parking options within the town or at designated starting points for popular trails, making it convenient to access the hiking routes.

Q: What is the difficulty level of the Camino de Santiago section near Villafranca Montes De Oca?
A: The Camino de Santiago section through the Montes de Oca, particularly after Villafranca, is known for its 'steep and long climb' at times, leading to the Alto de la Pedraja. While it offers moderate sections, hikers should be prepared for significant uphill portions and varied terrain, including forests and potentially misty paths.

Q: Are there any recreation areas or facilities along the trails?
A: Yes, for those looking for a place to rest or enjoy a picnic, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6315800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarcia Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; is available. It provides facilities amidst the natural surroundings, making it a convenient stop during your hike.

Access road to the stairs of the Alba dam. Quite a surprise!
Its car park is the starting point for several hiking trails.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55) is a moderate hike that generally takes around 3 hours and 8 minutes to complete. This estimate is for the 10.5 km route itself, so plan accordingly for your pace and any stops you might make.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse terrain, impressively combining the narrow and spectacular Oca gorge (desfiladero) with the beautiful forested landscapes of the Montes de Oca mountains. You'll encounter ancestral paths, including sections that are part of the historic Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: Where does the Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55) start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins at the Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Oca in Villafranca Montes de Oca. Parking is typically available near the hermitage, which serves as a convenient starting point for the route.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons to hike this trail. The weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery, especially the forested areas, is particularly vibrant. Summer can be pleasant, but always check local temperatures, while winter might present challenges with colder weather and potential snow in higher sections.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', the trail is suitable for regular hikers. While not overly strenuous, its length of 10.5 km and varied terrain might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. Families with some hiking experience should find it enjoyable, but always assess your group's fitness levels.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55)?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on the Alto Oca Trail. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in forested areas or near wildlife, and to ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or natural features can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in both natural beauty and historical significance. You'll experience the dramatic Oca gorge and serene forests. Notable highlights include the Beech Forest of the Alba Dam, the Access to Presa de Alba via the Canyon, and the historic Hermitage of the Virgin of Oca at the starting point.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55) utilizes ancestral paths, including sections of the historic Camino de Santiago. It also shares parts of its route with other local trails such as the Camino de Alba and the Desfiladero de la Hoz.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in the Montes de Oca area?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55) or access the Montes de Oca region. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Are there any circular route options for this trail?
faqs.answer: The Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55) is designed as a circular route, starting and ending at the Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Oca. This allows for a convenient and complete hiking experience without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and duration, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device (like the komoot app) is also advisable, along with sun protection.
